title: Idempotent Consumers, Inbox Records, and External Effects · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol; preserve message identity and timing, locate the first broken ownership boundary, and retain a deterministic regression case. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in durable inbox and duplicate-safe effect protocol and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Derive a stable scoped operation identity from producer intent, tenant, message type, and semantic version rather than delivery metadata.
Record inbox identity, input fingerprint, state transition, domain effect, and completion atomically in one database transaction where possible.
Reject reuse of an operation identity with a different payload fingerprint or semantic intent and retain conflict evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Inbox Retention Replay
steps.instruction:
Order the Inbox Retention Replay RabbitMQ and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Inbox Retention Replay.

content.text: A debug messaging team handles case 4952 in a benefit-notification consumer near its database pool limit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject fingerprint conflicts, suppress completed duplicates, and preserve external ambiguity; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.

Competency probe: Set inbox retention from maximum redelivery, replay, audit, privacy, and disaster-recovery windows and test expired-key behavior.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Publish the incident record · Debug Inbox Retention Replay, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Inbox records can be deleted immediately after acknowledgement because RabbitMQ will not redeliver.
